bids.reasoning: Since it is the beginning of the negotiation, I aim to propose an offer that maximizes my utility. At this stage, there is no need to make concessions as there is ample time to explore other possible offers and negotiations. I will select the value with the highest utility for each issue, given that there are no previous offers from the opponent to consider or respond to.

Considering the current time fraction is very early in the negotiation (0.02), I aim to maximize utility by proposing a bid close to my top-utility preferences. The opponent's first offer is significantly suboptimal for me, indicating they are starting with a low concession strategy. To maintain negotiation momentum, I will offer a high-utility combination for myself while being aware that high time pressure is not yet a factor. At this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.04), I am aiming to maximize my own utility by proposing offers with high utility for me. To establish a fairer negotiation range, I continue to offer my highest utility bid. This helps solidify my position before further gauging their willingness to move during the mid-game phase.

My utility from the current offers has been consistently high with little mutual agreement from the opponent. The opponent's bids have not shown an increase in utility to me, remaining quite low. The opponent is consistently offering low-utility bids for me, mainly focusing on value A, which is least preferred across issues based on my utility preferences. Despite their messages indicating a willingness to collaborate, their offers show rigidity.
